data.title: Seagate, Western Digital in focus as Wedbush ups price targets amid strong demand
data.description:
Seagate Technologies (NASDAQ:STX) and Western Digital (NASDAQ:WDC) were in focus on Thursday as Wedbush Securities upped its price target on the storage companies, citing continued strong demand.

Seagate shares rose 3% in premarket trading, while Western Digital was up nearly 2%.

“Given an underlying shortfall in supply that appears unlikely to be alleviated for 2+ years, we believe pricing and margins are nearly certain to continue an upward trajectory through CY2026 and most likely through 2027,” analyst Matt Bryson wrote in a note to clients. Bryson, who has an Outperform rating on both Seagate and Western Digital, raised his price targets to $260 and $135 from $175 and $90, respectively.

Delving deeper, Bryson said the upcoming quarterly reports from the pair should see “modest” beat and raises when compared to Wall Street estimates, largely due to better pricing and margins than their respective initial guidances indicated.

“On a relative scale, we anticipate WDC could deliver greater outperformance vs. STX for the quarter/guide, given somewhat better feedback on volumes, more room for WDC to mix up, and an easier bar (albeit we see both vendors as having provided guidance they should have been able to beat),” Bryson explained. “But given the substantial gains for both stocks over the past month or so, we also wouldn't be surprised if earnings prove slightly underwhelming if we are correct and both companies deliver incremental upside, vs. fundamentally game changing results and guidance this earnings season.”

Seagate is set to report quarterly results after the close of trading on October 28. A consensus of analysts expect the company to earn $2.40 per share on $2.55B in revenue.

Western Digital will report its quarterly results after the close of trading on October 30. A consensus of analysts expect it to earn $1.58 per share on $2.73B in sales.

data.title: Roadzen’s DrivebuddyAI signs six Indian e-commerce trucking fleets
data.description:
• Roadzen (NASDAQ:RDZN) said on Thursday its in-cabin intelligence platform, DrivebuddyAI, has secured contracts with six small and mid-sized trucking fleets in India to deploy its driver monitoring and collision warning systems across more than 1,500 commercial vehicles.
  • The five-year agreements, covering hardware installation and monthly software licensing, are valued in the mid-seven figures, the company said. Full deployment is expected by March 2026.
  • The fleets, including Rajam Logistics, DHIR Logistics and ARL Logistics, operate Volvo and BharatBenz trucks that serve major e-commerce firms.
  • Roadzen said each vehicle will connect to its 24×7 command centre for real-time driver monitoring and accident prevention.
  • RDZN +2.17% premarket to $1.41.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Cycurion announces five new contracts totalling $1M
data.description:
• Cycurion (NASDAQ:CYCU) on Thursday announced the closure of five new contracts in September, totaling approximately $1 million in value.
  • These agreements are expected to generate an additional $75,000 in monthly recurring revenue, bolstering the company’s growth trajectory.
  • The contracts include four in the healthcare and pharmaceutical industries.
  • CYCU +12.02% premarket to $0.2925.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Chagee Holdings uses high-profile flagship stores to promote its brand globally
data.description:
Chagee Holdings (NASDAQ:CHA) continues to accelerate its global growth strategy in 2025 with major flagship openings and an expanded international footprint. The Chinese tea brand is pursuing aggressive expansion across Asia and into new Western markets.

Recent high-profile store openings include two flagship stores in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia; the first U.S. opening in Los Angeles; and the brand unveiling its largest-ever global flagship in Hong Kong.

So far in 2025, Chagee's (NASDAQ:CHA) total global store count has climbed to more than 7K, marking a 65% year-over-year increase fueled by its franchising model and international market push. The company plans to open between 1,000 and 1,500 new stores globally this year, expanding both in China and abroad. Overseas, its footprint nearly doubled to 208 stores from 115 last year.

Chagee (CHA) currently operates stores in China, Malaysia, Singapore, Thailand, Indonesia, Vietnam, and the United States.

Shares of Chagee (CHA) closed at $16.45 on Wednesday after the IPO was priced at $28 per share.

data.title: Xenon appoints Tucker Kelly as CFO
data.description:
• Xenon Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:XENE) announced on Thursday the  appointment of Tucker Kelly as Chief Financial Officer and member of the Xenon senior executive team. 
  • Kelly is an executive who brings extensive strategic and commercial finance expertise, both in the U.S. and internationally, that will help the company prepare for the anticipated commercialization of its lead candidate, azetukalner. 
  • Azetukalner is currently being studied broadly in three Phase 3 programs for the treatment of epilepsy, major depressive disorder, and bipolar depression. 
  • Kelly most recently served as executive vice president, CFO, and treasurer at Deciphera Pharmaceuticals, now a wholly owned subsidiary of Ono Pharmaceuticals.

data.title: Golden Triangle ventures begins $10M restructuring to eliminate convertible debt
data.description:
• Golden Triangle Ventures (OTCPK:GTVH) on Thursday said it has begun a restructuring plan to eliminate all remaining convertible debt, retire 1.4 billion shares, reduce authorized shares, and introduce a structured buyback program.
  • The company said the agreement would remove more than $10M in combined debt and equity overhang from the company’s capitalization table.
  • The restructuring includes a clawback of approximately two billion shares previously issued through legacy debt conversions.
  • Through a definitive agreement with the company’s noteholder, 680 million shares will be issued as part of a final settlement to retire roughly $7 million in convertible debt, with 1.4 billion shares being retired and permanently removed from the company’s capitalization table.
  • The company confirmed it is reducing its authorized share count to seven billion to start, reinforcing capital discipline as it begins the next phase — the preferred equity cleanup.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Cloudastructure announces up to $5M share repurchase program
data.description:
• Cloudastructure (NASDAQ:CSAI) on Thursday announced a share repurchase program under which the company may repurchase up to $5 million of its outstanding shares of common stock in the open market.
  • CSAI +15.0% premarket to $1.61

data.title: Veritone dips on $75M equity offering
data.description:
• Veritone (NASDAQ:VERI) announced a $75 million registered direct offering, selling about 12.86 million shares at $5.83 per share.
  • The deal, led by Needham & Company, is expected to close around October 17, 2025, pending customary conditions.
  • Veritone plans to use the proceeds for working capital, debt repayment, and general corporate purposes.
  • VERI --4.6% premarket to $8.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Salesforce, Google expand partnership to increase focus on AI, expand Gemini access
data.description:
Salesforce (NYSE:CRM) and Alphabet's Google (NASDAQ:GOOGL) (NASDAQ:GOOG) announced an expansion of their existing partnership on Thursday to focus on artificial intelligence, specifically bringing the search giant's Gemini AI models to the former's Agentforce 360 Platform.

Salesforce shares rose more than 6% in premarket trading amid optimism surrounding its annual Dreamforce event, while Alphabet shares rose 0.6%.

As part of the deal, Google's Gemini models will come to more of its Workspace software suite and work with Agentforce 360. This allows sales and IT services to have access to Google's latest models, and Salesforce's Atlas reasoning engine, the companies said in a statement. 

The deal will also see Gemini come to Salesforce-owned Slack, via the work management software's search API. This is particularly noteworthy, given that OpenAI (OPENAI) recently unveiled AgentKit, aimed at making it easier for workflow automation to integrate into ChatGPT.

“In the enterprise environment, it’s imperative for AI agents to be highly capable and highly consistent, especially for critical use cases,” said Silvio Savarese, Chief Scientist at Salesforce, in a statement. “Together, we are setting a new standard for building the future of what’s possible in the Agentic Enterprise down to the model level.”

“The deeper integration of Google Workspace with Gemini and Salesforce has the power to fundamentally improve how teams get work done,” said George Kwon, VP of Product Management for Google Workspace Platform and Growth. “This integration unifies critical business information with powerful AI assistance, helping teams across sales, customer service, and IT make smarter decisions and streamline their work with critical information, all from the tools they use every day.”

On Wednesday, Salesforce said it expects revenue by fiscal 2030 to hit $60B, above analysts' estimates.
